stories.title: Microsoft Merges Enterprise and Consumer Copilot Apps
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft is combining its AI assistant Copilot into one app instead of having separate versions for home and business users.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Microsoft products, you might notice changes to where you find the AI assistant, but it should work the same way.

stories.title: Anthropic, White House Have Not Discussed Government Stake in Firm
stories.simple_summary: The White House and Anthropic (an AI company) have not discussed the government buying a stake in the company.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and company owners — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Tesla Pumps Brakes on In-House AI Usage
stories.simple_summary: Tesla is slowing down how much it uses its own artificial intelligence tools in its business.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to Tesla investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
stories.simple_summary: Some wealthy families are using AI programs to teach their children instead of sending them to regular schools.
stories.why_it_matters: Most people still don't trust AI with their children's education, but this shows a growing trend among the rich that may eventually influence education choices.

stories.title: The First Half of 2026 Is Over. These 2 Spectacular Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks Can Soar in the Second Half.
stories.simple_summary: An investment article is recommending certain AI company stocks they think will perform well in the second half of 2026.
stories.why_it_matters: This is only for people thinking about buying stocks — ordinary people and small business owners don't need to do anything.

stories.title: Prediction: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Could Double Before 2026 Ends
stories.simple_summary: An investment advisor predicts a certain AI company's stock price could double before the end of 2026.
stories.why_it_matters: This is only relevant if you're investing in stocks — nothing you need to act on otherwise.

stories.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is stopping new sign-ups for Mechanical Turk, which is a service where people do small computer tasks to earn money.
stories.why_it_matters: If you were thinking of earning extra money this way, you won't be able to join, though existing workers can keep going for now.

stories.title: Forget Micron? This New AI Memory Stock Could Be the Best Buy of the Decade
stories.simple_summary: An investment article is promoting a new AI memory company as a great stock to buy.
stories.why_it_matters: This is investment advice for stock buyers — nothing you need to act on unless you're investing.

stories.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
stories.simple_summary: Google made a funny advertisement imagining what would happen if the Founding Fathers had used Google's AI tools to write the Declaration of Independence.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just advertising — it doesn't affect you or your business.

stories.title: Philosophers Are the Latest Hiring Target for AI Companies
stories.simple_summary: AI companies are now hiring philosophers (people who think deeply about right and wrong) to help them make better decisions.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows that AI companies are starting to care more about fairness and ethics, which could mean safer AI products in the future.

stories.title: Palo Alto Networks and Koi Security sued over alleged AI error in cyber threat report
stories.simple_summary: Two companies are being sued because their AI tools made a mistake in a report about a cyber threat (a computer security problem).
stories.why_it_matters: This shows that AI is not perfect and mistakes can cause real problems, which is why it's important not to trust AI completely with important decisions.

stories.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
stories.simple_summary: Google created an advertisement imagining how the Founding Fathers might have used Google's collaboration tools and AI if they existed in 1776.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just an advertisement for Google's business tools — it doesn't affect you personally.
